items.title: Cross-validation evaluation protocol
items.description: k-fold construction, stratified folds, repeated k-fold, leakage-safe pipelines, and preprocessing per fold are canonical under Model Evaluation. This topic applies that machinery to model selection and tuning—not re-deriving fold algorithms.

items.title: Train/validation/test splits
items.description: CV for mitigation typically runs on the development block (train+val) while a locked test set waits for one post-selection evaluation. Split discipline prevents peeking at the test set during tuning.

items.title: Ridge and Lasso
items.description: Regularization strength α is commonly chosen by inner CV over a log-spaced grid. CV compares bias–variance tradeoffs across folds instead of overfitting penalty choice to one validation slice.

items.title: Random forests
items.description: Tree depth, `min_samples_leaf`, and `max_features` are frequently ranked with stratified CV metrics. Stable mean AUC or RMSE across folds helps avoid overfit deep trees favored by a lucky partition.
